frames: The agent begins in a very low posture, with its body nearly touching the ground and its four legs splayed outwards.
Its central body starts to lift slightly, and the legs begin to bend more acutely underneath it.
The agent's body continues to rise, with all four legs maintaining contact and adjusting their angles.
The body is now significantly elevated from the ground, supported by its four bent legs.
It continues to extend its legs, further raising its central body towards an upright position.
The agent reaches its highest posture, with its body well clear of the ground and legs extended downwards.
It holds this elevated stance, appearing stable and balanced on all four limbs.
The agent maintains its raised, static posture, showing no further movement.
